Modern celebrations can also help people stay connected across distance and make festivals more inclusive than before.
components.chinese: 很多年前，節日通常是以一種較固定、也較本地化的方式來慶祝的。現在，人們會從世界各地借用不同元素，而科技更是徹底改變了整個節日氣氛。社交媒體、網上訊息、視像通話，以及數碼照片，都在影響人們如何經歷節日。今天的節日不只是用來慶祝，也同時是人們會發佈、分享，甚至精心呈現的東西。這可能令它在表面上看起來更熱鬧、更盛大，但有時在內裡卻可能薄了一點。不過，若說所有改變都不好，也並不公平。現代的慶祝方式其實也能幫助人們跨越距離保持聯繫，並且令節日比以前更具包容性。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Years ago, festivals were often celebrated in a more fixed and
local way. Now, people borrow ideas from all over the place, and technology has
changed the whole atmosphere. Social media, online messages, video calls, and
digital photos all shape how festivals are experienced. A festival today is not just
something people celebrate; it is also something they post, share, and curate.
That can make it feel brighter and bigger on the surface, but sometimes a little
thinner underneath. Still, it would be unfair to say that all change is bad.
Modern celebrations can also help people stay connected across distance and
make festivals more inclusive than before.
[很多年前，節日通常是以一種較固定、也較本地化的方式來慶祝的。現在，人們會從世界
各地借用不同元素，而科技更是徹底改變了整個節日氣氛。社交媒體、網上訊息、視像通話
，以及數碼照片，都在影響人們如何經歷節日。今天的節日不只是用來慶祝，也同時是人們
會發佈、分享，甚至精心呈現的東西。這可能令它在表面上看起來更熱鬧、更盛大，但有時
在內裡卻可能薄了一點。不過，若說所有改變都不好，也並不公平。現代的慶祝方式其實也
能幫助人們跨越距離保持聯繫，並且令節日比以前更具包容性。]

components.english: For instance, in the past, if family members lived far away, they might simply miss the celebration altogether. Now, even if they cannot come home, they can still join a video call, send greetings instantly, and share photos in real time. In the same way, younger people today may celebrate both traditional festivals and imported ones, such as Christmas or Halloween, which makes festival culture feel more mixed and more global than it used to.
components.chinese: 例如，在過去，如果家庭成員住得很遠，他們可能就真的會完全錯過節日聚會。現在，即使他們不能回家，也仍然可以透過視像通話參與、即時送上祝福，並且同步分享照片。同樣地，今天的年輕人也可能同時慶祝傳統節日和外來節日，例如聖誕節或萬聖節，這令節日文化比以前更混合，也更全球化。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, in the past, if family members lived far away, they
might simply miss the celebration altogether. Now, even if they cannot come
home, they can still join a video call, send greetings instantly, and share photos
in real time. In the same way, younger people today may celebrate both
traditional festivals and imported ones, such as Christmas or Halloween, which
makes festival culture feel more mixed and more global than it used to.
[例如，在過去，如果家庭成員住得很遠，他們可能就真的會完全錯過節日聚會。現在，即使
他們不能回家，也仍然可以透過視像通話參與、即時送上祝福，並且同步分享照片。同樣地
，今天的年輕人也可能同時慶祝傳統節日和外來節日，例如聖誕節或萬聖節，這令節日文化
比以前更混合，也更全球化。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say festivals in the past were often simpler, slower, and more tradition-bound, whereas festivals today are usually more convenient, more commercial, and more shaped by technology. However, the core purpose has not disappeared. People still celebrate festivals for the same reason, really: to feel connected, to mark something meaningful, and to bring a little light into ordinary life.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，過去的節日通常較簡單、較慢，也更受傳統規範；而今天的節日則往往更方便、更商業化，也更受科技影響。不過，節日最核心的目的並沒有消失。說到底，人們慶祝節日仍然是為了同樣的原因：去感受連結、去標記一些有意義的時刻，以及為平凡生活添一點光。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say festivals in the past were often simpler, slower,
and more tradition-bound, whereas festivals today are usually more convenient,
more commercial, and more shaped by technology. However, the core purpose
has not disappeared. People still celebrate festivals for the same reason, really: to
feel connected, to mark something meaningful, and to bring a little light into
ordinary life.
[總結來說，我會說，過去的節日通常較簡單、較慢，也更受傳統規範；而今天的節日則往往
更方便、更商業化，也更受科技影響。不過，節日最核心的目的並沒有消失。說到底，人們慶
祝節日仍然是為了同樣的原因：去感受連結、去標記一些有意義的時刻，以及為平凡生活添
一點光。]

question.english: Do you think festivals like Christmas are replacing traditional festivals in your country?
question.chinese: 你認為像聖誕節這樣的節日，正在取代你國家的傳統節日嗎？

source_lines.text: Do you think festivals like Christmas are replacing traditional festivals in your country?
[主題：你認為像聖誕節這樣的節日，正在取代你國家的傳統節日嗎？]

components.english: Personally, no, I do not think Christmas is replacing traditional festivals in Hong Kong. If anything, it has been added to the mix rather than taking over the whole stage.
components.chinese: 就我個人而言，不，我並不認為聖誕節正在取代香港的傳統節日。若要說得更準確一點，它比較像是被加進了整體節日文化之中，而不是把整個舞台都搶走了。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, no, I do not think Christmas is replacing traditional festivals
in Hong Kong. If anything, it has been added to the mix rather than taking over
the whole stage.
[就我個人而言，不，我並不認為聖誕節正在取代香港的傳統節日。若要說得更準確一點，
它比較像是被加進了整體節日文化之中，而不是把整個舞台都搶走了。]

components.english: The reason is that traditional festivals still have deep roots in family life, cultural memory, and the public calendar. In Hong Kong, official holidays still include major traditional festivals such as Lunar New Year, Tuen Ng, Mid-Autumn-related holidays, and Chung Yeung, while Christmas is also widely observed. So the picture is not one of replacement, but one of coexistence. Christmas may sparkle in the shop windows, but traditional festivals still sit closer to the family table.
components.chinese: 原因是，傳統節日在家庭生活、文化記憶，以及公共日曆裡仍然扎根很深。在香港，官方假期仍然包括農曆新年、端午節、中秋節相關假期，以及重陽節等主要傳統節日，而聖誕節同樣也被廣泛慶祝。所以，整體情況並不是一種「被取代」，而更像是一種「並存」。聖誕節也許在商店櫥窗裡閃閃發亮，但傳統節日仍然更靠近家庭的飯桌。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The reason is that traditional festivals still have deep roots in
family life, cultural memory, and the public calendar. In Hong Kong, official
holidays still include major traditional festivals such as Lunar New Year, Tuen
Ng, Mid-Autumn-related holidays, and Chung Yeung, while Christmas is also
widely observed. So the picture is not one of replacement, but one of
coexistence. Christmas may sparkle in the shop windows, but traditional
festivals still sit closer to the family table.
[原因是，傳統節日在家庭生活、文化記憶，以及公共日曆裡仍然扎根很深。在香港，官方假
期仍然包括農曆新年、端午節、中秋節相關假期，以及重陽節等主要傳統節日，而聖誕節同
樣也被廣泛慶祝。所以，整體情況並不是一種「被取代」，而更像是一種「並存」。聖誕節也許
在商店櫥窗裡閃閃發亮，但傳統節日仍然更靠近家庭的飯桌。]

components.english: For example, many people in Hong Kong enjoy Christmas lights, seasonal decorations, and gatherings with friends, but when traditional festivals come around, families still pay close attention to customs and reunion. People may celebrate Christmas for the atmosphere, but they often hold on to traditional festivals for the sense of identity and continuity.
components.chinese: 例如，很多香港人都會享受聖誕燈飾、節日裝飾，以及和朋友相聚的氣氛；但當傳統節日來到時，家庭仍然會很重視習俗和團聚。人們也許是為了氣氛而慶祝聖誕節，但他們往往是為了身份認同和文化延續而緊握傳統節日。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, many people in Hong Kong enjoy Christmas lights,
seasonal decorations, and gatherings with friends, but when traditional festivals
come around, families still pay close attention to customs and reunion. People
may celebrate Christmas for the atmosphere, but they often hold on to
traditional festivals for the sense of identity and continuity.
[例如，很多香港人都會享受聖誕燈飾、節日裝飾，以及和朋友相聚的氣氛；但當傳統節日
來到時，家庭仍然會很重視習俗和團聚。人們也許是為了氣氛而慶祝聖誕節，但他們往往是
為了身份認同和文化延續而緊握傳統節日。]

components.english: So, overall, I would say Christmas is not replacing traditional festivals; it is simply sharing space with them in a more globalised society.
components.chinese: 所以，整體來說，我會說聖誕節並不是在取代傳統節日；它只是在人更全球化的社會裡，和它們共享同一片空間。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, overall, I would say Christmas is not replacing traditional
festivals; it is simply sharing space with them in a more globalised society.
[所以，整體來說，我會說聖誕節並不是在取代傳統節日；它只是在人更全球化的社會裡，
和它們共享同一片空間。]

components.english: That said, I do think festivals like Christmas can sometimes overshadow traditional ones in the commercial and visual sense, especially for younger people.
components.chinese: 不過，我的確認為，像聖誕節這樣的節日有時會在商業層面和視覺層面上蓋過傳統節日，尤其對年輕人來說更是如此。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do think festivals like Christmas can sometimes
overshadow traditional ones in the commercial and visual sense, especially for
younger people.
[不過，我的確認為，像聖誕節這樣的節日有時會在商業層面和視覺層面上蓋過傳統節日，
尤其對年輕人來說更是如此。]

components.english: Christmas is highly visible. It comes with lights, music, gift-giving, shopping campaigns, and a strong social atmosphere, so it is easy for it to capture attention. By contrast, some traditional festivals are quieter, more symbolic, and more tied to family rituals than public spectacle. So while they may not be disappearing, they can sometimes lose ground in terms of excitement and visibility. In other words, the roots are still there, but the spotlight is not always equally shared.
components.chinese: 聖誕節的可見度非常高。它伴隨著燈飾、音樂、送禮、購物宣傳，以及很強的社交氣氛，所以很容易吸引人注意。相比之下，有些傳統節日則較安靜、較象徵化，也更偏向家庭儀式，而不是公共表演。所以，雖然它們未必正在消失，但在熱鬧程度和可見度方面，有時的確會稍微失去一些地盤。換句話說，根仍然在，只是聚光燈未必總是平均照著每一邊。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Christmas is highly visible. It comes with lights, music,
gift-giving, shopping campaigns, and a strong social atmosphere, so it is easy for
it to capture attention. By contrast, some traditional festivals are quieter, more
symbolic, and more tied to family rituals than public spectacle. So while they
may not be disappearing, they can sometimes lose ground in terms of excitement
and visibility. In other words, the roots are still there, but the spotlight is not
always equally shared.
[聖誕節的可見度非常高。它伴隨著燈飾、音樂、送禮、購物宣傳，以及很強的社交氣氛，所
以很容易吸引人注意。相比之下，有些傳統節日則較安靜、較象徵化，也更偏向家庭儀式，
而不是公共表演。所以，雖然它們未必正在消失，但在熱鬧程度和可見度方面，有時的確會
稍微失去一些地盤。換句話說，根仍然在，只是聚光燈未必總是平均照著每一邊。]

components.english: For instance, some young people may get more excited about Christmas parties, exchanging gifts, or taking photos with festive decorations than about the customs behind a traditional festival. However, that often depends on family influence. If parents and grandparents keep those traditions alive, children are much more likely to grow up valuing both.
components.chinese: 例如，有些年輕人可能會對聖誕派對、交換禮物，或者和節日裝飾拍照更感興趣，而對傳統節日背後的習俗反而沒有那麼投入。不過，這往往也取決於家庭影響。如果父母和祖父母能把那些傳統延續下去，孩子通常就更有可能同時珍惜兩者。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, some young people may get more excited about
Christmas parties, exchanging gifts, or taking photos with festive decorations
than about the customs behind a traditional festival. However, that often
depends on family influence. If parents and grandparents keep those traditions
alive, children are much more likely to grow up valuing both.
[例如，有些年輕人可能會對聖誕派對、交換禮物，或者和節日裝飾拍照更感興趣，而對傳
統節日背後的習俗反而沒有那麼投入。不過，這往往也取決於家庭影響。如果父母和祖父母
能把那些傳統延續下去，孩子通常就更有可能同時珍惜兩者。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say Christmas is not replacing traditional festivals in Hong Kong, but it is competing more strongly for attention. Traditional festivals still carry the deeper cultural heartbeat; Christmas simply adds another rhythm to the calendar.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說聖誕節並沒有在香港取代傳統節日，但它的確更強烈地在爭奪人們的注意力。傳統節日仍然承載著更深的文化脈搏；而聖誕節，只是為整個年曆再加上一種不同的節奏。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say Christmas is not replacing traditional festivals
in Hong Kong, but it is competing more strongly for attention. Traditional
festivals still carry the deeper cultural heartbeat; Christmas simply adds another
rhythm to the calendar.
[總結來說，我會說聖誕節並沒有在香港取代傳統節日，但它的確更強烈地在爭奪人們的注
意力。傳統節日仍然承載著更深的文化脈搏；而聖誕節，只是為整個年曆再加上一種不同的
節奏。]

question.english: Do you think it is wrong for children not to celebrate traditional festivals?
question.chinese: 你認為孩子不慶祝傳統節日是錯的嗎？

source_lines.text: Do you think it is wrong for children not to celebrate traditional festivals?
你認為孩子不慶祝傳統節日是錯的嗎？

components.english: Personally, I would not say it is simply wrong, but I do think it is a pity if children grow up completely disconnected from traditional festivals.
components.chinese: 就我個人而言，我不會直接說那一定是錯的，但如果孩子在成長過程中和傳統節日完全脫節，我的確會覺得有點可惜。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, I would not say it is simply wrong, but I do think it is a pity
if children grow up completely disconnected from traditional festivals.
[就我個人而言，我不會直接說那一定是錯的，但如果孩子在成長過程中和傳統節日完全脫
節，我的確會覺得有點可惜。]

components.english: The reason is that traditional festivals are not just dates on a calendar. They carry stories, values, family memories, and a sense of cultural belonging. In a way, they are part of a child’s roots. If children never take part in them at all, they may miss out on something deeper than just food or decoration; they may miss a connection to where they come from.
components.chinese: 原因在於，傳統節日並不只是日曆上的某幾天而已。它們承載著故事、價值觀、家庭回憶，以及一種文化歸屬感。某程度上，它們就像孩子成長中的根。如果孩子從來都不參與，他們錯過的其實不只是食物或裝飾，而是和自己來處的一種連結。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The reason is that traditional festivals are not just dates on a
calendar. They carry stories, values, family memories, and a sense of cultural
belonging. In a way, they are part of a child’s roots. If children never take part in
them at all, they may miss out on something deeper than just food or
decoration; they may miss a connection to where they come from.
[原因在於，傳統節日並不只是日曆上的某幾天而已。它們承載著故事、價值觀、家庭回憶，
以及一種文化歸屬感。某程度上，它們就像孩子成長中的根。如果孩子從來都不參與，他們
錯過的其實不只是食物或裝飾，而是和自己來處的一種連結。]

components.english: For example, if a child joins family members during Lunar New Year or Mid-Autumn Festival, they do not just enjoy the festive food; they also hear family stories, learn small customs, and feel the warmth of being part of something larger than themselves. Those moments may seem small, but they often stay with people for years.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個孩子在農曆新年或中秋節時和家人一起過節，他得到的其實不只是節日食物的快樂；同時他也會聽到一些家族故事、學到一些小習俗，並感受到自己是更大整體的一部分。這些時刻看起來也許很小，但往往會在一個人心裡留很多年。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a child joins family members during Lunar New
Year or Mid-Autumn Festival, they do not just enjoy the festive food; they also
hear family stories, learn small customs, and feel the warmth of being part of
something larger than themselves. Those moments may seem small, but they
often stay with people for years.
[例如，如果一個孩子在農曆新年或中秋節時和家人一起過節，他得到的其實不只是節日食
物的快樂；同時他也會聽到一些家族故事、學到一些小習俗，並感受到自己是更大整體的一
部分。這些時刻看起來也許很小，但往往會在一個人心裡留很多年。]

components.english: So, I would say it is not exactly wrong, but it is definitely something worth encouraging, because traditional festivals can help children build a stronger sense of identity and belonging.
components.chinese: 所以，我會說這不算是絕對的錯，但的確是值得鼓勵的事，因為傳統節日能幫助孩子建立更強的身份認同感和歸屬感。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, I would say it is not exactly wrong, but it is definitely
something worth encouraging, because traditional festivals can help children
build a stronger sense of identity and belonging.
[所以，我會說這不算是絕對的錯，但的確是值得鼓勵的事，因為傳統節日能幫助孩子建立
更強的身份認同感和歸屬感。]

components.english: That said, I do not think children should be forced to celebrate traditional festivals in a rigid or mechanical way.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為孩子應該被用很僵硬、很機械化的方式逼著去慶祝傳統節日。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think children should be forced to celebrate
traditional festivals in a rigid or mechanical way.
[不過，我也不認為孩子應該被用很僵硬、很機械化的方式逼著去慶祝傳統節日。]

components.english: If celebration turns into pure obligation, the heart can easily go out of it. Children are much more likely to appreciate tradition when it is introduced naturally, with warmth and meaning, rather than as a box they have to tick. After all, tradition should be a bridge, not a burden. What really matters is that children understand why the festival matters, not that they perform every custom perfectly.
components.chinese: 如果慶祝最後變成一種純粹的責任，那節日原本的味道就很容易消失。對孩子來說，如果傳統是用自然、有溫度、有意義的方式被帶進生活裡，他們通常會更容易欣賞它，而不是把它當成一個必須完成的項目。畢竟，傳統應該是一座橋，而不應該是一個負擔。真正重要的是，孩子明白這個節日為甚麼重要，而不是把每一個習俗都表演得完美無缺。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: If celebration turns into pure obligation, the heart can easily
go out of it. Children are much more likely to appreciate tradition when it is
introduced naturally, with warmth and meaning, rather than as a box they have
to tick. After all, tradition should be a bridge, not a burden. What really
matters is that children understand why the festival matters, not that they
perform every custom perfectly.
[如果慶祝最後變成一種純粹的責任，那節日原本的味道就很容易消失。對孩子來說，如果
傳統是用自然、有溫度、有意義的方式被帶進生活裡，他們通常會更容易欣賞它，而不是把
它當成一個必須完成的項目。畢竟，傳統應該是一座橋，而不應該是一個負擔。真正重要的
是，孩子明白這個節日為甚麼重要，而不是把每一個習俗都表演得完美無缺。]

components.english: For instance, a child may not enjoy every formal custom, but they may still connect with the festival by helping to make festive food, carrying a lantern, or spending time with grandparents. That kind of experience feels far more alive than simply being told, “You must do this because it is tradition.”
components.chinese: 例如，一個孩子未必會喜歡每一種很正式的習俗，但他仍然可能透過幫忙做節日食物、提燈籠，或者陪祖父母一起過節，而和那個節日建立連結。這種經驗通常會比單純被告知「因為這是傳統，所以你一定要做」來得更真實、更有生命力。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a child may not enjoy every formal custom, but they
may still connect with the festival by helping to make festive food, carrying a
lantern, or spending time with grandparents. That kind of experience feels far
more alive than simply being told, “You must do this because it is tradition.”
[例如，一個孩子未必會喜歡每一種很正式的習俗，但他仍然可能透過幫忙做節日食物、提
燈籠，或者陪祖父母一起過節，而和那個節日建立連結。這種經驗通常會比單純被告知「因
為這是傳統，所以你一定要做」來得更真實、更有生命力。]

components.english: Overall, I do not think it is fair to say children are wrong if they do not celebrate traditional festivals, but I do think it is a loss if they never get the chance to engage with them. The best approach, in my view, is not to force tradition on children, but to let them grow into it and feel its value for themselves.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我不認為可以簡單地說，孩子如果不慶祝傳統節日就是錯的；但如果他們從來沒有機會接觸和參與，那的確是一種可惜。在我看來，最好的做法不是把傳統硬塞給孩子，而是讓他們慢慢走進去，自己感受到當中的價值。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I do not think it is fair to say children are wrong if
they do not celebrate traditional festivals, but I do think it is a loss if they never
get the chance to engage with them. The best approach, in my view, is not to
force tradition on children, but to let them grow into it and feel its value for
themselves.
[總結來說，我不認為可以簡單地說，孩子如果不慶祝傳統節日就是錯的；但如果他們從來
沒有機會接觸和參與，那的確是一種可惜。在我看來，最好的做法不是把傳統硬塞給孩子，
而是讓他們慢慢走進去，自己感受到當中的價值。]

question.english: Do you think teens should learn about traditions?
question.chinese: 你認為青少年應該學習傳統嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think teens should learn about traditions?
你認為青少年應該學習傳統嗎？

components.english: Yes, I definitely think they should, because traditions help young people understand where they come from and what has shaped their community.
components.chinese: 是的，我的確認為他們應該學習，因為傳統能幫助年輕人明白自己從哪裡來，以及甚麼塑造了他們所屬的社群。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I definitely think they should, because traditions help young people
understand where they come from and what has shaped their community.
[是的，我的確認為他們應該學習，因為傳統能幫助年輕人明白自己從哪裡來，以及甚麼塑
造了他們所屬的社群。]

components.english: The main reason is that traditions carry more than just old customs; they also carry memory, values, and a sense of identity. If teenagers grow up knowing nothing about the festivals, stories, etiquette, or family practices around them, they may end up feeling culturally adrift, as if they know how to move forward but not where their roots are. In that sense, traditions are a bit like a bridge between generations: they connect the past to the present and stop culture from being washed away by time.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，傳統承載的不只是古老習俗，還有記憶、價值觀，以及一種身份認同。如果青少年在成長過程中，對身邊的節日、故事、禮儀，或者家庭習慣一無所知，他們可能會變得有點文化上失根，好像只知道怎樣往前走，卻不知道自己的根在哪裡。從這個角度來看，傳統有點像一座連接不同世代的橋樑：它把過去和現在連起來，也避免文化被時間慢慢沖淡。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that traditions carry more than just old
customs; they also carry memory, values, and a sense of identity. If teenagers
grow up knowing nothing about the festivals, stories, etiquette, or family
practices around them, they may end up feeling culturally adrift, as if they
know how to move forward but not where their roots are. In that sense,
traditions are a bit like a bridge between generations: they connect the past to
the present and stop culture from being washed away by time.
[主要原因是，傳統承載的不只是古老習俗，還有記憶、價值觀，以及一種身份認同。如果青
少年在成長過程中，對身邊的節日、故事、禮儀，或者家庭習慣一無所知，他們可能會變得
有點文化上失根，好像只知道怎樣往前走，卻不知道自己的根在哪裡。從這個角度來看，傳
統有點像一座連接不同世代的橋樑：它把過去和現在連起來，也避免文化被時間慢慢沖

components.english: For example, when teenagers learn about traditional festivals, family rituals, or even the meaning behind certain foods and greetings, they often start to see that these things are not random. A Lunar New Year meal, a Mid-Autumn gathering, or a simple act of showing respect to elders can teach them something about gratitude, belonging, and shared history.
components.chinese: 例如，當青少年去了解傳統節日、家庭習俗，甚至某些食物和問候方式背後的意義時，他們往往會開始明白，這些事情並不是毫無原因的。一頓農曆新年團年飯、一場中秋相聚，或者一個尊重長輩的簡單舉動，都可以讓他們學到感恩、歸屬感，以及共同歷史的重要性。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, when teenagers learn about traditional festivals,
family rituals, or even the meaning behind certain foods and greetings, they
often start to see that these things are not random. A Lunar New Year meal, a
Mid-Autumn gathering, or a simple act of showing respect to elders can teach
them something about gratitude, belonging, and shared history.
[例如，當青少年去了解傳統節日、家庭習俗，甚至某些食物和問候方式背後的意義時，他
們往往會開始明白，這些事情並不是毫無原因的。一頓農曆新年團年飯、一場中秋相聚，或
者一個尊重長輩的簡單舉動，都可以讓他們學到感恩、歸屬感，以及共同歷史的重要性。]

components.english: So yes, I would say teenagers should learn about traditions, because they give young people a stronger sense of identity and a deeper connection to the people around them.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說青少年應該學習傳統，因為這能讓他們建立更強的身份認同，也能和身邊的人產生更深的連結。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say teenagers should learn about traditions,
because they give young people a stronger sense of identity and a deeper
connection to the people around them.
[所以，是的，我會說青少年應該學習傳統，因為這能讓他們建立更強的身份認同，也能和
身邊的人產生更深的連結。]

components.english: That said, I do not think traditions should be taught in a rigid or unquestioning way.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為傳統應該以一種僵化、或者不容質疑的方式去教導。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think traditions should be taught in a rigid or
unquestioning way.
[不過，我也不認為傳統應該以一種僵化、或者不容質疑的方式去教導。]

components.english: To be honest, if traditions are presented as a list of rules that must simply be obeyed, many teenagers will switch off straight away. Young people are more likely to engage with tradition when they understand the meaning behind it and are allowed to think critically about it. After all, not every tradition ages well. Some deserve to be preserved, while others may need to be adapted to fit modern life. In other words, tradition should be a living thread, not a chain around people’s ankles.
components.chinese: 老實說，如果傳統只是被呈現成一串必須服從的規矩，很多青少年很快就會失去興趣。當年輕人能夠明白傳統背後的意義，並被允許用批判思考去看待它時，他們才更有可能真正投入其中。畢竟，並不是每一種傳統都能原封不動地適應今天的世界。有些值得保留，但有些則可能需要調整，才能配合現代生活。換句話說，傳統應該是一條活生生的線，而不是綁住人腳步的鎖鏈。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, if traditions are presented as a list of rules that
must simply be obeyed, many teenagers will switch off straight away. Young
people are more likely to engage with tradition when they understand the
meaning behind it and are allowed to think critically about it. After all, not
every tradition ages well. Some deserve to be preserved, while others may need
to be adapted to fit modern life. In other words, tradition should be a living
thread, not a chain around people’s ankles.
[老實說，如果傳統只是被呈現成一串必須服從的規矩，很多青少年很快就會失去興趣。當
年輕人能夠明白傳統背後的意義，並被允許用批判思考去看待它時，他們才更有可能真正
投入其中。畢竟，並不是每一種傳統都能原封不動地適應今天的世界。有些值得保留，但有
些則可能需要調整，才能配合現代生活。換句話說，傳統應該是一條活生生的線，而不是綁
住人腳步的鎖鏈。]

components.english: For instance, a teenager may enjoy learning the story behind a traditional festival or the symbolism of a family custom, even if they do not follow every part of it exactly. In that case, they are not rejecting tradition; they are engaging with it in a thoughtful way rather than going through the motions.
components.chinese: 例如，一個青少年也許會很有興趣了解某個傳統節日背後的故事，或者某個家庭習俗所代表的象徵意義，即使他未必每一個細節都照做。在這種情況下，他並不是在拒絕傳統；相反，他是在以一種有思考的方式去接觸它，而不是只是形式上地敷衍跟從。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a teenager may enjoy learning the story behind a
traditional festival or the symbolism of a family custom, even if they do not
follow every part of it exactly. In that case, they are not rejecting tradition; they
are engaging with it in a thoughtful way rather than going through the
motions.
[例如，一個青少年也許會很有興趣了解某個傳統節日背後的故事，或者某個家庭習俗所代
表的象徵意義，即使他未必每一個細節都照做。在這種情況下，他並不是在拒絕傳統；相反
，他是在以一種有思考的方式去接觸它，而不是只是形式上地敷衍跟從。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say teens should absolutely learn about traditions, but they should be encouraged to understand them rather than just inherit them blindly. The best traditions do not trap young people in the past; they give them roots while still leaving room to grow.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說青少年絕對應該學習傳統，但更重要的是要鼓勵他們真正理解，而不是盲目地照單全收。最好的傳統，不是把年輕人困在過去，而是在給他們根的同時，也留下成長的空間。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say teens should absolutely learn about traditions,
but they should be encouraged to understand them rather than just inherit
them blindly. The best traditions do not trap young people in the past; they give
them roots while still leaving room to grow.
[總結來說，我會說青少年絕對應該學習傳統，但更重要的是要鼓勵他們真正理解，而不是
盲目地照單全收。最好的傳統，不是把年輕人困在過去，而是在給他們根的同時，也留下成
長的空間。]

question.english: Do you think the traditions in your country are restrictive, especially for women in any sense?
question.chinese: 你認為你地方的傳統是否在某些方面具有限制性，特別是對女性而言？

source_lines.text: Do you think the traditions in your country are restrictive, especially for women in any
sense?
主題：你認為你地方的傳統是否在某些方面具有限制性，特別是對女性而言？

components.english: Yes, I would say some traditions can still be restrictive, especially for women, although the situation is much less rigid than it used to be.
components.chinese: 是的，我會說有些傳統在今天仍然可能帶有一定限制性，特別是對女性而言，不過整體情況已經比以前沒有那麼僵化了。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I would say some traditions can still be restrictive, especially for
women, although the situation is much less rigid than it used to be.
[是的，我會說有些傳統在今天仍然可能帶有一定限制性，特別是對女性而言，不過整體情
況已經比以前沒有那麼僵化了。]

components.english: In a place like Hong Kong, people are generally more modern and open-minded now, but some older expectations still linger in the background. Women may still be judged more heavily on things like marriage, childbearing, appearance, or how well they balance work and family. In that sense, tradition can sometimes feel like an invisible script: no one may force it on you directly, but you can still feel the pressure to play the role properly. The restriction is not always loud or obvious; quite often, it is subtle, social, and deeply ingrained.
components.chinese: 在像香港這樣的地方，人們現在整體上已經現代和開放得多，但一些較舊的觀念仍然會留在背景之中。女性有時仍然會在婚姻、生育、外表，或者是否能同時兼顧工作與家庭這些事情上，被更嚴格地評價。從這個角度來看，傳統有時像一套看不見的劇本：未必有人明確逼你照著做，但你仍然會感受到一種要把角色演好的壓力。這種限制未必總是大聲而明顯的；很多時候，它其實是很細微、很社會化，而且根深蒂固的。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: In a place like Hong Kong, people are generally more modern
and open-minded now, but some older expectations still linger in the
background. Women may still be judged more heavily on things like marriage,
childbearing, appearance, or how well they balance work and family. In that
sense, tradition can sometimes feel like an invisible script: no one may force it on
you directly, but you can still feel the pressure to play the role properly. The
restriction is not always loud or obvious; quite often, it is subtle, social, and
deeply ingrained.
[在像香港這樣的地方，人們現在整體上已經現代和開放得多，但一些較舊的觀念仍然會留
在背景之中。女性有時仍然會在婚姻、生育、外表，或者是否能同時兼顧工作與家庭這些事
情上，被更嚴格地評價。從這個角度來看，傳統有時像一套看不見的劇本：未必有人明確逼
你照著做，但你仍然會感受到一種要把角色演好的壓力。這種限制未必總是大聲而明顯的；
很多時候，它其實是很細微、很社會化，而且根深蒂固的。]

components.english: For example, a woman who focuses strongly on her career may still be asked when she plans to get married or have children, whereas a man in the same position is often asked far less. In the same way, if a woman is ambitious or outspoken, some people may admire her, but others may see her as too strong or not traditional enough. That double standard has not disappeared overnight.
components.chinese: 例如，一位非常專注事業的女性，現在仍然可能經常被問到甚麼時候結婚、甚麼時候生孩子；但如果是同樣情況的男性，通常就較少受到這類追問。同樣地，如果一位女性很有野心，或者表達得很直接，有些人會欣賞她，但也有些人可能會覺得她太強勢，或者不夠傳統。這種雙重標準並不是一夜之間就消失了。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a woman who focuses strongly on her career may still
be asked when she plans to get married or have children, whereas a man in the
same position is often asked far less. In the same way, if a woman is ambitious or
outspoken, some people may admire her, but others may see her as too strong or
not traditional enough. That double standard has not disappeared overnight.
[例如，一位非常專注事業的女性，現在仍然可能經常被問到甚麼時候結婚、甚麼時候生孩
子；但如果是同樣情況的男性，通常就較少受到這類追問。同樣地，如果一位女性很有野心
，或者表達得很直接，有些人會欣賞她，但也有些人可能會覺得她太強勢，或者不夠傳統。
這種雙重標準並不是一夜之間就消失了。]

components.english: So yes, I do think some traditions can still place limits on women, not always through strict rules, but through expectations that quietly shape how they are judged and how they live.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我的確認為有些傳統到現在仍然會對女性造成某程度上的限制，不一定是透過明文規則，而是透過一些無形的期待，慢慢影響她們如何被看待，以及如何生活。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I do think some traditions can still place limits on
women, not always through strict rules, but through expectations that quietly
shape how they are judged and how they live.
[所以，是的，我的確認為有些傳統到現在仍然會對女性造成某程度上的限制，不一定是透
過明文規則，而是透過一些無形的期待，慢慢影響她們如何被看待，以及如何生活。]

components.english: That said, I do not think tradition itself is always the problem, because many traditions also carry warmth, identity, and a sense of belonging.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為傳統本身一定就是問題，因為很多傳統同時也承載著溫度、身份認同，以及歸屬感。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think tradition itself is always the problem,
because many traditions also carry warmth, identity, and a sense of belonging.
[不過，我也不認為傳統本身一定就是問題，因為很多傳統同時也承載著溫度、身份認同，
以及歸屬感。]

components.english: To be honest, the real issue arises when traditions become too rigid or are used as a measuring stick for how a woman should live her life. Respect for family, caring for elders, and staying connected to cultural values can all be positive things. The problem begins when those values harden into narrow expectations, as if there were only one proper way to be a daughter, a wife, or a mother. Tradition can be a root, which gives people strength, but it should not become a cage.
components.chinese: 老實說，真正的問題通常出現在傳統變得過於僵硬，或者被拿來當作衡量女性應該怎樣生活的一把尺的時候。重視家庭、照顧長輩，以及和文化價值保持連結，這些本身都可以是很正面的事情。問題是，當這些價值慢慢硬化成一套很狹窄的期待，好像女性只能用某一種方式去做女兒、做太太、或者做母親，那就會出問題。傳統可以是一種根，讓人站得更穩，但它不應該變成一個籠。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, the real issue arises when traditions become too
rigid or are used as a measuring stick for how a woman should live her life.
Respect for family, caring for elders, and staying connected to cultural values
can all be positive things. The problem begins when those values harden into
narrow expectations, as if there were only one proper way to be a daughter, a
wife, or a mother. Tradition can be a root, which gives people strength, but it
should not become a cage.
[老實說，真正的問題通常出現在傳統變得過於僵硬，或者被拿來當作衡量女性應該怎樣生
活的一把尺的時候。重視家庭、照顧長輩，以及和文化價值保持連結，這些本身都可以是很
正面的事情。問題是，當這些價值慢慢硬化成一套很狹窄的期待，好像女性只能用某一種方
式去做女兒、做太太、或者做母親，那就會出問題。傳統可以是一種根，讓人站得更穩，但它
不應該變成一個籠。]

components.english: For instance, celebrating festivals together, sharing family meals, or showing respect to older generations can be meaningful and beautiful. However, if those same family values are used to pressure women to sacrifice every personal goal, then something has gone off balance. In my view, culture should guide people, not box them in.
components.chinese: 例如，一家人一起過節、一起吃團年飯，或者尊重上一代，這些都可以是很有意義、也很美好的事情。不過，如果同樣的家庭價值被用來施壓女性，要她們放下所有個人目標去配合別人的期待，那就失去平衡了。在我看來，文化應該引導人，而不是把人困住。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, celebrating festivals together, sharing family meals,
or showing respect to older generations can be meaningful and beautiful.
However, if those same family values are used to pressure women to sacrifice
every personal goal, then something has gone off balance. In my view, culture
should guide people, not box them in.
[例如，一家人一起過節、一起吃團年飯，或者尊重上一代，這些都可以是很有意義、也很美
好的事情。不過，如果同樣的家庭價值被用來施壓女性，要她們放下所有個人目標去配合別
人的期待，那就失去平衡了。在我看來，文化應該引導人，而不是把人困住。]

components.english: Overall, I would say some traditions in my place can still be restrictive for women, but the problem is usually not tradition at its best; it is tradition when it is treated as fixed, one-sided, and beyond question. Ideally, tradition should not simply be thrown out altogether, but allowed to breathe with the times, so that it preserves its value while still leaving room for choice.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說我地方的一些傳統對女性來說仍然可能帶有限制性，不過真正的問題通常不是傳統最美好的一面，而是當傳統被當成一套固定、單向，而且不容質疑的標準時。最理想的情況不是把傳統一刀切地拋開，而是讓它隨時代一起呼吸，既保留價值，也留出選擇。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I would say some traditions in my place can still be
restrictive for women, but the problem is usually not tradition at its best; it is
tradition when it is treated as fixed, one-sided, and beyond question. Ideally,
tradition should not simply be thrown out altogether, but allowed to breathe
with the times, so that it preserves its value while still leaving room for choice.
